title: pedicularis rosea subsp. rosea
intro: Pedicularis rosea subsp. rosea, also known as Rose Lousewort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, particularly in the Mediterranean area. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Rose Lousewort include reducing inflammation, promoting digestion, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are typically consumed after being dried and processed, although its availability and usage may vary due to its limited cultivation and potential regulations.
